assumptions: Assume the question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9, not separate financial statements.
Assume the dividend is between entities within the same consolidated group and is denominated in a currency other than at least one entity's functional currency.
Assume the key technical issue is whether the related intragroup receivable is a monetary item whose foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
No facts are provided on whether the foreign exchange effects reach consolidated profit or loss; this is treated as the critical gating condition.

approaches.label: General prohibition on intragroup hedged items in consolidation
approaches.reasoning: In consolidated financial statements, hedged items must generally be exposures with parties external to the reporting entity. An intragroup dividend is therefore not eligible merely because it exists within the group.
approaches.practical_implication: Absent a specific IFRS 9 exception, the hedge documentation would not be supportable.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraph 6.3.5 limits hedged items in consolidation to external-party assets, liabilities, firm commitments and highly probable forecast transactions, subject to narrow exceptions.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge of a recognized intragroup monetary receivable
approaches.reasoning: A recognized intragroup monetary item may qualify in consolidated financial statements if it creates foreign exchange gains or losses that are not fully eliminated on consolidation. For a recognized receivable, the relevant hedge model is a fair value hedge of the foreign currency risk.
conditions: The dividend receivable is a monetary intragroup item
The entities involved have different functional currencies
The foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation
The foreign exchange risk affects consolidated profit or loss
The hedging relationship is formally designated and documented at inception and meets hedge effectiveness requirements
approaches.practical_implication: The hedge can be documented only if the receivable's FX exposure survives into consolidated profit or loss.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraph 6.3.6 permits foreign currency risk on intragroup monetary items when gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation; paragraphs 6.3.2, 6.4.1 and 6.5.2(a) add measurability, documentation and fair value hedge requirements.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of a forecast intragroup dividend
approaches.reasoning: A highly probable forecast intragroup transaction can qualify only if it is denominated in a foreign currency and its foreign exchange risk will affect consolidated profit or loss. On reasonable assumptions, an intragroup dividend would often fail that profit-or-loss condition.
conditions: The dividend is still a highly probable forecast transaction rather than an already recognized receivable
It is denominated in a currency other than the functional currency of the entity entering into the transaction
The foreign exchange risk will affect consolidated profit or loss
The hedging relationship is formally designated and documented at inception and meets hedge effectiveness requirements
approaches.practical_implication: Unless a clear consolidated profit-or-loss effect can be demonstrated, a dividend hedge should not be documented as a cash flow hedge.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraph 6.3.6 allows certain forecast intragroup transactions in consolidation, and paragraph B6.3.5 states they qualify only when the foreign currency risk affects consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge of a foreign operation
approaches.reasoning: This hedge model applies to foreign currency risk on the net assets of a foreign operation, not to an intragroup dividend receivable. The hedged item is the net investment amount, not a declared internal distribution.
approaches.practical_implication: If the objective is to hedge a dividend receivable, net investment hedge accounting is the wrong model.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraph 6.5.2 identifies a hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model.
Paragraphs 10–14 confine the hedged risk to qualifying functional-currency differences on a net investment and the hedged item to net assets, not an intragroup dividend receivable.
